metadata.reviewBoundary: Automated reconciliation is structural validation only; it is not human linguistic or pedagogical review, approval, activation, or runtime truth.

morphemes.gloss: can be done (after a base word)

rootArtifacts.microLore: An actor is a do-er; a reaction is a doing-back.
A biography is life-writing; biology is the study of life.
A Roman centurion led one hundred soldiers.
A dictionary is where the words are 'said'; a verdict is a 'truth-saying'.
Gaia was the Greek earth goddess — geo- words map her world.
Greek scholars wrote on scrolls; an autograph is self-writing.
The Hydra was a water-serpent; hydro- words all flow from water.
Perimeter: the measure around; geometry: earth-measuring.
Perimeter: the measure around; geometry: earth-measuring.
A microscope makes the small world visible.
Your nation is where you were born; nature is everything born into the world.
The nymph Echo could only repeat sounds — phon is her root.
A photograph is light-writing — drawing with light itself.
A polygon has many corners; polyphonic music has many voices.
Roman merchants carried goods to the port — the carrying-place.
When Vesuvius erupted, it BROKE out of the mountain.
Greek lookouts watched from towers; a telescope is a far-looker.
Roman scribes wrote everything by hand.
Roman scribes wrote everything by hand.
Roman engineers were master builders — roads, walls, aqueducts.
Greek messengers ran far — now tele- words carry sound and sight far.
Greek hot springs were called thermae — the Romans borrowed them for their baths.
A tractor pulls; subtract pulls a number away.
A trident has three points — Poseidon's three-toothed spear.

rootAliasModel.variantGroupKey: groups written variants and aliases without slash-delimited identity keys
rootAliasModel.variantOf: points variant entries to the primary reusable identity
